author: śrīla rūpa gosvāmī
verses:
viracaya mayi daṇḍaṁ dīna⋅bandho dayāṁ vā
gatir iha na bhavattaḥ kācid anyā mamāsti
nipatatu śatakoṭir nirmalaṁ vā navāmbhas
tad api kila payodaḥ stūyate cātakena

prācīnānāṁ bhajanam atulaṁ duṣkaraṁ śṛṇvato me
nairāśyena jvalati hṛdayaṁ bhakti⋅leśālasasya
viṣvadrīcīm aghahara tavākarṇya kāruṇya⋅vīcīm
āśā⋅vindūkṣitam idam upaity antare hanta śaityam
en_translation:
O friend of the distressed! Whether You punish me or show me mercy, I have no other refuge but You. Even if a hundred million thunderbolts or pure fresh rain should fall, the chataka bird still praises the cloud.

As I hear of the incomparable and difficult worship of the ancient devotees, my heart—devoid of even a trace of devotion—burns with despair. But upon hearing of Your all-pervading wave of compassion, O remover of sins, this heart, sprinkled with a drop of hope, attains coolness within.
